root_text.an3.99:1.1: “Navopi, bhikkhave, potthako dubbaṇṇo ca hoti dukkhasamphasso ca appaggho ca;
root_text.an3.99:1.2: majjhimopi, bhikkhave, potthako dubbaṇṇo ca hoti dukkhasamphasso ca appaggho ca;
root_text.an3.99:1.3: jiṇṇopi, bhikkhave, potthako dubbaṇṇo ca hoti dukkhasamphasso ca appaggho ca.
root_text.an3.99:1.4: Jiṇṇampi, bhikkhave, potthakaṁ ukkhaliparimajjanaṁ vā karonti saṅkārakūṭe vā naṁ chaḍḍenti.
root_text.an3.99:2.1: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, navo cepi bhikkhu hoti dussīlo pāpadhammo.
root_text.an3.99:2.3: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, potthako dubbaṇṇo tathūpamāhaṁ, bhikkhave, imaṁ puggalaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.an3.99:2.4: Ye kho panassa sevanti bhajanti payirupāsanti diṭṭhānugatiṁ āpajjanti, tesaṁ taṁ hoti dīgharattaṁ ahitāya dukkhāya.
root_text.an3.99:2.6: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, potthako dukkhasamphasso tathūpamāhaṁ, bhikkhave, imaṁ puggalaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.an3.99:2.7: Yesaṁ kho pana so paṭiggaṇhāti cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhāraṁ, tesaṁ taṁ na mahapphalaṁ hoti na mahānisaṁsaṁ.
root_text.an3.99:2.9: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, potthako appaggho tathūpamāhaṁ, bhikkhave, imaṁ puggalaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.an3.99:2.11: thero cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu hoti dussīlo pāpadhammo, idamassa dubbaṇṇatāya vadāmi.
root_text.an3.99:2.12: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, potthako dubbaṇṇo tathūpamāhaṁ, bhikkhave, imaṁ puggalaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.an3.99:2.13: Ye kho panassa sevanti bhajanti payirupāsanti diṭṭhānugatiṁ āpajjanti, tesaṁ taṁ hoti dīgharattaṁ ahitāya dukkhāya.
root_text.an3.99:2.15: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, potthako dukkhasamphasso tathūpamāhaṁ, bhikkhave, imaṁ puggalaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.an3.99:2.16: Yesaṁ kho pana so paṭiggaṇhāti cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhāraṁ, tesaṁ taṁ na mahapphalaṁ hoti na mahānisaṁsaṁ.
root_text.an3.99:2.18: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, potthako appaggho tathūpamāhaṁ, bhikkhave, imaṁ puggalaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.an3.99:3.1: Evarūpo cāyaṁ, bhikkhave, thero bhikkhu saṅghamajjhe bhaṇati.
root_text.an3.99:3.3: ‘kiṁ nu kho tuyhaṁ bālassa abyattassa bhaṇitena,
root_text.an3.99:3.5: So kupito anattamano tathārūpiṁ vācaṁ nicchāreti yathārūpāya vācāya saṅgho taṁ ukkhipati, saṅkārakūṭeva naṁ potthakaṁ.
root_text.an3.99:4.1: Navampi, bhikkhave, kāsikaṁ vatthaṁ vaṇṇavantañceva hoti sukhasamphassañca mahagghañca; majjhimampi, bhikkhave, kāsikaṁ vatthaṁ vaṇṇavantañceva hoti sukhasamphassañca mahagghañca; jiṇṇampi, bhikkhave, kāsikaṁ vatthaṁ vaṇṇavantañceva hoti sukhasamphassañca mahagghañca.
root_text.an3.99:4.2: Jiṇṇampi, bhikkhave, kāsikaṁ vatthaṁ ratanapaliveṭhanaṁ vā karoti gandhakaraṇḍake vā naṁ pakkhipanti.
root_text.an3.99:5.1: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, navo cepi bhikkhu hoti sīlavā kalyāṇadhammo, idamassa suvaṇṇatāya vadāmi.
root_text.an3.99:5.2: Seyyathāpi taṁ, bhikkhave, kāsikaṁ vatthaṁ vaṇṇavantaṁ tathūpamāhaṁ, bhikkhave, imaṁ puggalaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.an3.99:5.3: Ye kho panassa sevanti bhajanti payirupāsanti diṭṭhānugatiṁ āpajjanti, tesaṁ taṁ hoti dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāya.
root_text.an3.99:5.5: Seyyathāpi taṁ, bhikkhave, kāsikaṁ vatthaṁ sukhasamphassaṁ tathūpamāhaṁ, bhikkhave, imaṁ puggalaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.an3.99:5.6: Yesaṁ kho pana so paṭiggaṇhāti cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhāraṁ, tesaṁ taṁ mahapphalaṁ hoti mahānisaṁsaṁ.
root_text.an3.99:5.8: Seyyathāpi taṁ, bhikkhave, kāsikaṁ vatthaṁ mahagghaṁ tathūpamāhaṁ, bhikkhave, imaṁ puggalaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.an3.99:5.10: thero cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu hoti …pe… puggalaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.an3.99:6.1: Evarūpo cāyaṁ, bhikkhave, thero bhikkhu saṅghamajjhe bhaṇati.
root_text.an3.99:6.3: ‘appasaddā āyasmanto hotha, thero bhikkhu dhammañca vinayañca bhaṇatī’ti.

translation_text.an3.99:1.1: “Jute canvas is ugly, unpleasant to touch, and worthless whether it’s new, worn in, or worn out.
translation_text.an3.99:1.4: They use worn out jute canvas for scrubbing cauldrons, or else they just throw it away on the rubbish heap.
translation_text.an3.99:2.1: In the same way, if a junior mendicant is unethical, of bad character,
translation_text.an3.99:2.2: this is how they’re ugly, I say.
translation_text.an3.99:2.3: That individual is just as ugly as jute canvas.
translation_text.an3.99:2.4: If you associate with, accompany, and attend to them, following their example, it’ll be for your lasting harm and suffering.
translation_text.an3.99:2.5: This is how they’re unpleasant to touch, I say.
translation_text.an3.99:2.6: That individual is just as unpleasant to touch as jute canvas.
translation_text.an3.99:2.7: Any robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick that they receive are not very fruitful or beneficial for the donor.
translation_text.an3.99:2.8: This is how they’re worthless, I say.
translation_text.an3.99:2.9: That individual is just as worthless as jute canvas.
translation_text.an3.99:2.10: If a middle mendicant is unethical, of bad character, this is how they’re ugly, I say. …
translation_text.an3.99:2.11: If a senior mendicant is unethical, of bad character, this is how they’re ugly, I say. …
translation_text.an3.99:2.13: If you associate with, accompany, and attend to them, following their example, it’ll be for your lasting harm and suffering. …
translation_text.an3.99:3.1: If such a senior mendicant speaks among the Saṅgha,
translation_text.an3.99:3.3: ‘What’s an incompetent fool like you got to say?
translation_text.an3.99:3.4: How on earth could you imagine you’ve got something worth saying!’
translation_text.an3.99:3.5: They become angry and upset, and blurt out things that make the Saṅgha throw them out, as if they were throwing jute canvas away on the rubbish heap.
translation_text.an3.99:4.1: Cloth from Kāsi is beautiful, pleasant to touch, and valuable whether it’s new, worn in, or worn out.
translation_text.an3.99:4.2: They use worn out cloth from Kāsi for wrapping, or else they place it in a fragrant casket.
translation_text.an3.99:5.1: In the same way, if a junior mendicant is ethical, of good character, this is how they’re beautiful, I say.
translation_text.an3.99:5.2: That individual is just as beautiful as cloth from Kāsi.
translation_text.an3.99:5.3: If you associate with, accompany, and attend to such an individual, following their example, it will be for your lasting welfare and happiness.
translation_text.an3.99:5.4: This is how they’re pleasant to touch, I say.
translation_text.an3.99:5.5: That individual is just as pleasant to touch as cloth from Kāsi.
translation_text.an3.99:5.6: Any robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick that they receive are very fruitful and beneficial for the donor.
translation_text.an3.99:5.7: This is how they’re valuable, I say.
translation_text.an3.99:5.8: That individual is just as valuable as cloth from Kāsi.
translation_text.an3.99:5.9: If a middle mendicant is ethical, of good character, this is how they’re beautiful, I say. …
translation_text.an3.99:5.10: If a senior mendicant is ethical, of good character, this is how they’re beautiful, I say. …
translation_text.an3.99:6.1: If such a senior mendicant speaks in the midst of the Saṅgha,
translation_text.an3.99:6.3: ‘Venerables, be quiet! The senior mendicant is speaking on the teaching and training.’
translation_text.an3.99:6.4: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an3.99:6.5: ‘We will be like cloth from Kāsi, not like jute canvas.’

variant_text.an3.99:2.7: Yesaṁ kho pana so → yesaṁ kho pana (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed); yesaṁ so (mr) | paṭiggaṇhāti → patigaṇhāti (bj, pts1ed)
